title: We are the Moshe of Jesus
summary: “And the child grew, and she brought him unto Pharaoh's daughter, and he became her son. And she called his name Moses: and she said, Because I drew him out of the water.” (Exodus 2:10).The water of the river Nile changed baby Moses’ destiny from that of a slave to a royal prince. Hallelujah! When Moses was put into the ark and lowered onto the waters of the river Nile, the hands that lowered the basked were those of a slave – Jochebed. The hands, however, which picked him out of the waters of the Nile were those of a royal princess – the daughter of Pharaoh (Heb 11:24). How did this incredible change in destiny happen? Between a life of slavery and the palace there was faith and water. “By faith Moses, when he was born, was hid three months of his parents, because they saw he was a proper child; and they were not afraid of the king's commandment” (Heb 11:23). When faith and water combine a miracle happens. I wish to talk to you today about the water that changes our destiny. There are some Jochebed here today: their children will be baptized in Jesus’ name. Like Jochebed you realized there is no future for your children no matter how much love you may shower upon them. “He that believeth and is baptized shall be saved; but he that believeth not shall be damned” (Mark 16:16). It is foolish to stay in the house of the damned; better to lower your child into the waters of baptism. Every good mother wants the best future for her child – then lower your child into the waters of baptism in Jesus’ name! The hands that will lift your child out of the waters on the other side in heaven are those of the Lord Jesus.
